info.name: BE_ONLINE_SHOP_ADHIVASINDO
info.description:
# BE_ONLINE_SHOP_ADHIVASINDO

API documentation for the **Online Shop Adhivasindo** backend application. This API is built with **Laravel** and uses **JWT (JSON Web Token)**-based authentication.

---

## 🚀 Getting Started

Follow these steps in order to use all endpoints correctly:

1. **Make sure the server is running** at `http://localhost:8000`
2. **Set the environment** to `Online Shop Adhivasindo` (or the appropriate environment)
3. **REGISTER** or **LOGIN** first — the JWT token will be automatically saved to the `{{token}}` variable
4. Once logged in, all endpoints that require authentication are ready to use

---

## 🔐 Authentication

All endpoints (except `REGISTER` and `LOGIN`) require a **Bearer Token** in the header:

```
Authorization: Bearer {{token}}
```

This token is automatically saved to the `{{token}}` environment variable after a successful login.

---

## 📌 Important Note on IDs

> ⚠️ **All IDs in this API use UUID format** (example: `a1b2c3d4-e5f6-7890-1234-567890abcdef`)

UUIDs **cannot be guessed or made up**. Here's how to get the correct IDs:
- **`type_id`** → Get it from the `GET /product-types` endpoint response (PRODUCT_TYPES folder)
- **`product_id`** → Get it from the `GET /products/` or `POST /products/` endpoint response (PRODUCTS folder)
- **`order_id`** → Get it from the `GET /orders/` or `POST /orders/` endpoint response (ORDERS folder)

---

## 📂 Collection Structure

| Folder | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `API_CHECKING` | Check whether the API server is running |
| `AUTHENTICATION` | Register, login, view profile, refresh token, logout |
| `PRODUCT_TYPES` | Manage product types / categories |
| `PRODUCTS` | Manage products (including image upload) |
| `ORDERS` | Manage orders / checkout |

---

## 🌐 Base URL

```
http://localhost:8000/api
```

Use the `{{apiUrl}}` variable in your environment to easily switch the base URL.

item.name: API_CHECKING

request.description:
Checks whether the API server is running normally. No authentication required.

**Endpoint:** `GET {{apiUrl}}/`

**Auth:** Not required

**Success Response (200 OK):**
```json
{
    "message": "API is running"
}
```

> ✅ A 200 response means the server is active and ready to use.
> ❌ If there is no response or a connection error, make sure the Laravel server is running (`php artisan serve`).

item.description: This folder contains the endpoint to check whether the API server is running normally (health check). Use this endpoint before making any other requests to confirm the server is active.

item.name: AUTHENTICATION

request.description:
Registers a new user account in the system.

**Endpoint:** `POST {{apiUrl}}/auth/register`

**Auth:** Not required

**Body (JSON):**
```json
{
    "name": "John Doe",
    "email": "john@example.com",
    "password": "password123",
    "password_confirmation": "password123"
}
```

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| name | string | Yes | Full name of the user |
| email | string | Yes | Email address (must be unique) |
| password | string | Yes | Password, minimum 8 characters |
| password_confirmation | string | Yes | Must match the `password` field |

**Success Response (201):** Account created successfully. Proceed with `LOGIN` to obtain a token.

Signs in to the system using email and password. On success, the JWT token is automatically saved to the `{{token}}` variable.

**Endpoint:** `POST {{apiUrl}}/auth/login`

**Auth:** Not required

**Body (JSON):**
```json
{
    "email": "john@example.com",
    "password": "password123"
}
```

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| email | string | Yes | Registered email address |
| password | string | Yes | Account password |

**Success Response (200):** JWT token is returned and automatically saved to `{{token}}`.

> ✅ After a successful login, you can immediately use all other endpoints that require authentication.

Retrieves the profile data of the currently logged-in user based on the active JWT token.

**Endpoint:** `GET {{apiUrl}}/auth/me`

**Auth:** Bearer Token (`{{token}}`)

**Success Response (200):** Returns the user's profile data such as `id`, `name`, `email`, and other information.

> 💡 Use this endpoint to verify that your token is still valid and to retrieve the currently active user's data.

Refreshes an expired or soon-to-expire JWT token without requiring a new login. The old token is replaced with a new one that is automatically saved to the `{{token}}` variable.

**Endpoint:** `POST {{apiUrl}}/auth/refresh`

**Auth:** Bearer Token (`{{token}}`)

**Body:** Not required

**Success Response (200):** A new token is returned and automatically replaces the existing `{{token}}`.

> ⏰ Use this endpoint when you receive a `401 Unauthorized` error due to an expired token, so you don't have to log in again from scratch.

Logs out of the system and invalidates the currently active JWT token. The `{{token}}` variable is automatically cleared after a successful logout.

**Endpoint:** `POST {{apiUrl}}/auth/logout`

**Auth:** Bearer Token (`{{token}}`)

**Body:** Not required

**Success Response (200):** Token successfully invalidated on the server side.

> ⚠️ After logging out, the old token can no longer be used. You must `LOGIN` again to obtain a new token.

item.description:
This folder contains all endpoints related to user authentication, including registration, login, viewing your profile, refreshing the token, and logging out.

**Recommended usage order:**
1. `REGISTER` — create a new account (if you don't have one)
2. `LOGIN` — sign in and receive a JWT token
3. `ME` — check your profile data
4. `REFRESH_TOKEN` — renew the token before it expires
5. `LOGOUT` — sign out and invalidate the token

> 🔑 After a successful `LOGIN`, the JWT token is automatically saved to the `{{token}}` variable and can be used immediately across all other endpoints.

item.name: PRODUCT_TYPES

request.description:
Retrieves a list of all available product types.

**Endpoint:** `GET {{apiUrl}}/product-types`

**Auth:** Bearer Token (`{{token}}`)

**Success Response (200):** Returns an array of all product types. Each item contains an `id` (UUID) and a `name`.

> 📋 **Save the UUID from this response!** That UUID is used as the `type_id` when creating or editing products in the PRODUCTS folder. UUIDs cannot be guessed — they must be taken from this endpoint's response.

Adds a new product type to the system.

**Endpoint:** `POST {{apiUrl}}/product-types`

**Auth:** Bearer Token (`{{token}}`)

**Body (JSON):**
```json
{
    "name": "Food"
}
```

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| name | string | Yes | Name of the product type / category |

**Success Response (201):** New product type created successfully. The response includes the `id` (UUID) of the newly created product type.

> 💡 Save the UUID from this response if you want to use it immediately as a `type_id` when creating a product.

Updates the name of an existing product type by its ID.

**Endpoint:** `PATCH {{apiUrl}}/product-types/:id`

**Auth:** Bearer Token (`{{token}}`)

**Path Parameter:**
| Parameter | Description |
|---|---|
| id | UUID of the product type to update. Get it from the `GET_PRODUCTS` response in this folder. |

> ⚠️ **ID uses UUID format.** Example: `a1b2c3d4-e5f6-7890-1234-567890abcdef`. Do not make up a UUID — always get it from the `GET_PRODUCTS` response.

**Body (JSON):**
```json
{
    "name": "Beverage"
}
```

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| name | string | Yes | New name for the product type |

**Success Response (200):** Returns the updated product type data.

Deletes a product type by its ID.

**Endpoint:** `DELETE {{apiUrl}}/product-types/:id`

**Auth:** Bearer Token (`{{token}}`)

**Path Parameter:**
| Parameter | Description |
|---|---|
| id | UUID of the product type to delete. Get it from the `GET_PRODUCTS` response in this folder. |

> ⚠️ **ID uses UUID format.** Do not make up a UUID — always get it from the `GET_PRODUCTS` response.

> 🚨 **Warning:** Deleting a product type may affect products that are currently using it. Make sure no active products are still assigned to this type before deleting.

**Success Response (200):** Product type deleted successfully.

item.description:
This folder contains endpoints for managing **product types / categories**. Product types are used as categories when creating or editing products.

> 📌 **Important:** Each product type has a UUID-formatted ID. This ID is required when creating or editing products in the PRODUCTS folder. Always retrieve the `type_id` from the `GET_PRODUCTS` endpoint response in this folder.

**Recommended usage order:**
1. `GET_PRODUCTS` — view all available product types and note their UUIDs
2. `POST_PRODUCT` — add a new product type
3. `EDIT_PRODUCT` — update the name of an existing product type
4. `DELETE_PRODUCT` — remove a product type

**Auth:** All endpoints in this folder require a Bearer Token (`{{token}}`).

item.name: PRODUCTS

request.description:
Retrieves a list of the best-selling products based on order quantities.

**Endpoint:** `GET {{apiUrl}}/products/best-sellers`

**Auth:** Not required (Optional Bearer Token)

**Query Parameters:**
| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| limit | integer | No | Number of best-selling products to return (default: `10`) |

**Example:** `GET {{apiUrl}}/products/best-sellers?limit=5`

**Success Response (200):** Returns a list of the top-selling products ordered by `sold_count`.

Retrieves a list of all products. Supports filtering by status and searching by name.

**Endpoint:** `GET {{apiUrl}}/products/`

**Auth:** Bearer Token (`{{token}}`)

**Query Parameters:**
| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| status | string | No | Filter by status: `active`, `inactive`, `draft`, `out_of_stock`, `low_stock` |
| search | string | No | Search products by name |
| per_page | integer | No | Number of results per page (e.g. `10`) |
| page | integer | No | Page number for pagination |

**Example:** `GET {{apiUrl}}/products/?status=active`

**Success Response (200):** Returns a paginated list of products. Each product has an `id` field (UUID).

> 📋 **Save the UUID from this response!** That UUID is used as the `product_id` in the `GET_PRODUCT`, `EDIT_PRODUCT`, and `DELETE_PRODUCT` endpoints.

Retrieves the full details of a single product by its ID.

**Endpoint:** `GET {{apiUrl}}/products/:id`

**Auth:** Bearer Token (`{{token}}`)

**Path Parameter:**
| Parameter | Description |
|---|---|
| id | UUID of the product to view. Use `{{product_id}}` or get it from the `GET_PRODUCTS` response. |

> ⚠️ **ID uses UUID format.** Example: `a1b2c3d4-e5f6-7890-1234-567890abcdef`. Do not make up a UUID — always get it from the `GET_PRODUCTS` response.

**Success Response (200):** Returns the full product data including name, price, stock, status, product type, and image URL (if any).

Creates a new product. On success, the new product's ID is automatically saved to the `{{product_id}}` variable.

**Endpoint:** `POST {{apiUrl}}/products/`

**Auth:** Bearer Token (`{{token}}`)

---

### Why form-data instead of JSON?

This endpoint uses **multipart/form-data** (not JSON) because it supports uploading a product image file. JSON format cannot be used to send binary files like images. With form-data, you can send both text fields and files in a single request.

---

**Body (form-data):**
| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| name | string | Yes | Product name |
| price | number | Yes | Product price |
| type_id | string | Yes | UUID of the product type. Get it from `GET_PRODUCTS` in the PRODUCT_TYPES folder. Use `{{type_id}}` |
| desc | string | No | Product description |
| stock | integer | No | Stock quantity (default: `0`) |
| status | string | No | Product status: `draft`, `active`, `inactive` (default: `draft`) |
| image | file | No | Product image file |

> ⚠️ **`type_id` uses UUID format** and must be retrieved from the `GET_PRODUCTS` endpoint in the PRODUCT_TYPES folder. Do not make up a UUID.

**Success Response (201):** Product created successfully. The new product's ID is automatically saved to `{{product_id}}`.

Updates an existing product by its ID.

**Endpoint:** `POST {{apiUrl}}/products/:id` (with `_method: PATCH`)

**Auth:** Bearer Token (`{{token}}`)

**Path Parameter:**
| Parameter | Description |
|---|---|
| id | UUID of the product to update. Use `{{product_id}}` or get it from the `GET_PRODUCTS` response. |

> ⚠️ **ID uses UUID format.** Do not make up a UUID — always get it from the `GET_PRODUCTS` response.

---

### Why form-data instead of JSON?

This endpoint uses **multipart/form-data** (not JSON) because it supports uploading or replacing a product image file. JSON format cannot be used to send binary files like images. With form-data, you can send both text fields and files in a single request.

---

### ⚠️ Notes: Method Spoofing with `_method`

Because form-data with file uploads can only be sent via `POST`, while Laravel expects the `PATCH` method for updates, **method spoofing** is used.

How it works: add a `_method` field with the value `PATCH` in the form-data body. Laravel will read this field and treat the request as if it were sent with the `PATCH` method.

> 🔴 **Required:** The `_method: PATCH` field must always be present in the body. Without it, the request will fail or not be processed correctly by Laravel.

---

**Body (form-data):**
| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| _method | string | **Yes** | Must be set to `PATCH` (for Laravel method spoofing) |
| name | string | No | Updated product name |
| price | number | No | Updated product price |
| type_id | string | No | Updated product type UUID |
| desc | string | No | Updated product description |
| stock | integer | No | Updated stock quantity |
| status | string | No | Updated status: `active`, `inactive`, `draft` |
| image | file | No | New product image file |

**Success Response (200):** Returns the updated product data.

Deletes a product by its ID. On success, the `{{product_id}}` variable is automatically cleared.

**Endpoint:** `DELETE {{apiUrl}}/products/:id`

**Auth:** Bearer Token (`{{token}}`)

**Path Parameter:**
| Parameter | Description |
|---|---|
| id | UUID of the product to delete. Use `{{product_id}}` or get it from the `GET_PRODUCTS` response. |

> ⚠️ **ID uses UUID format.** Do not make up a UUID — always get it from the `GET_PRODUCTS` response.

> 🚨 **Warning:** Product deletion is permanent. Make sure the product is not currently referenced in any active orders before deleting.

**Success Response (200):** Product deleted successfully.

item.description:
This folder contains endpoints for managing **products** in the online shop, including listing products, viewing product details, creating new products, editing, and deleting products.

> 📌 **Important:** Each product has a UUID-formatted ID. Always retrieve the `product_id` from the `GET_PRODUCTS` or `CREATE_PRODUCT` endpoint response.

> 🖼️ **Image Upload Note:** The `CREATE_PRODUCT` and `EDIT_PRODUCT` endpoints use **form-data** (not JSON) because they support uploading a product image file. JSON format cannot be used to send files.

**Recommended usage order:**
1. `GET_PRODUCTS` — view all products
2. `GET_PRODUCT` — view details of a single product
3. `CREATE_PRODUCT` — add a new product (with or without an image)
4. `EDIT_PRODUCT` — update an existing product
5. `DELETE_PRODUCT` — remove a product

**Auth:** All endpoints in this folder require a Bearer Token (`{{token}}`).

item.name: ORDERS

request.description:
Places a new order (checkout). On success, the new order's ID is automatically saved to the `{{order_id}}` variable.

**Endpoint:** `POST {{apiUrl}}/orders/`

**Auth:** Bearer Token (`{{token}}`)

**Body (JSON):**
```json
{
    "payment_method": "cash",
    "items": [
        {
            "product_id": "{{product_id_active}}",
            "quantity": 2
        }
    ]
}
```

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| payment_method | string | Yes | Payment method (e.g. `cash`) |
| items | array | Yes | List of products to order |
| items[].product_id | string | Yes | UUID of the product. Get it from `GET_PRODUCTS` in the PRODUCTS folder. |
| items[].quantity | integer | Yes | Quantity to order |

> ⚠️ **`product_id` uses UUID format** and must be retrieved from the `GET_PRODUCTS` endpoint in the PRODUCTS folder. Do not make up a UUID.

> 💡 You can order more than one product at a time by adding more items to the `items` array.

**Success Response (201):** Order placed successfully. The new order's ID is automatically saved to `{{order_id}}`.

Updates the status of an existing order by its ID. Use this endpoint to transition an order through its lifecycle (e.g. from `pending` to `paid`).

**Endpoint:** `PATCH {{apiUrl}}/orders/:id/status`

**Auth:** Bearer Token (`{{token}}`)

**Path Parameter:**
| Parameter | Description |
|---|---|
| id | UUID of the order to update. Use `{{order_id}}` or get it from the `GET_ORDERS` response. |

> ⚠️ **ID uses UUID format.** Do not make up a UUID — always get it from the `GET_ORDERS` or `CHECKOUT` response.

**Body (JSON):**
```json
{
    "status": "paid"
}
```

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| status | string | Yes | New order status (e.g. `pending`, `paid`, `cancelled`) |

**Success Response (200):** Returns the order data with the updated status.

Retrieves a paginated list of all orders belonging to the currently logged-in user.

**Endpoint:** `GET {{apiUrl}}/orders/`

**Auth:** Bearer Token (`{{token}}`)

**Query Parameters:**
| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| per_page | integer | No | Number of orders per page (e.g. `10`) |
| page | integer | No | Page number for pagination |

**Success Response (200):** Returns a paginated list of orders. Each order has an `id` field (UUID).

> 📋 **Save the UUID from this response!** That UUID is used as the `order_id` in the `GET_ORDER` and `UPDATE_STATUS` endpoints.

Retrieves the full details of a single order by its ID.

**Endpoint:** `GET {{apiUrl}}/orders/:id`

**Auth:** Bearer Token (`{{token}}`)

**Path Parameter:**
| Parameter | Description |
|---|---|
| id | UUID of the order to view. Use `{{order_id}}` or get it from the `GET_ORDERS` response. |

> ⚠️ **ID uses UUID format.** Example: `a1b2c3d4-e5f6-7890-1234-567890abcdef`. Do not make up a UUID — always get it from the `GET_ORDERS` or `CHECKOUT` response.

**Success Response (200):** Returns the full order data including the list of ordered items, total price, payment method, and order status.

item.description:
This folder contains endpoints for managing **orders** in the online shop, including placing a checkout, listing orders, viewing order details, and updating order status.

> 📌 **Important:** Each order has a UUID-formatted ID. Always retrieve the `order_id` from the `GET_ORDERS` or `CHECKOUT` endpoint response.

**Recommended usage order:**
1. `CHECKOUT` — place a new order (make sure you already have a `product_id` from the PRODUCTS folder)
2. `GET_ORDERS` — view all orders
3. `GET_ORDER` — view details of a single order
4. `UPDATE_STATUS` — update the status of an order

**Auth:** All endpoints in this folder require a Bearer Token (`{{token}}`).
